key_insight: Gradient clipping makes asynchronous and federated SGD robust to slow workers by removing the oracle-complexity dependence on the maximum delay.
engineering_relevance: For distributed training systems, clipping can improve stability and scalability under stragglers without requiring tighter synchronization.
method: Model asynchronous SGD with delayed updates from heterogeneous workers.
Analyze clipped gradients under a sub-Weibull noise model to cover heavy-tailed gradient noise.
Prove convergence in expectation and high-probability bounds that no longer depend on maximum delay.
evidence_signal: The paper provides theoretical convergence guarantees—both in expectation and with high probability—rather than reporting an empirical benchmark in the abstract.
watch_item: The result depends on choosing an appropriate clipping threshold, which may require tuning to balance robustness and bias.
paper_title: Clipping Makes Distributed and Federated Asynchronous SGD Robust to Stragglers
